# Project Lighthouse — Design Sync
NOTE: This is an AI-generated test document, it's not real, and any resemblance to real people or places is coincidence.

**Date:** 2026-05-18
**Attendees:** Elena Torres (Design Lead), Sarah Chen (Product Manager), David Kim (Stakeholder)

## Agenda
- Review warehouse naming conventions from David's team.
- Walk through updated multi-warehouse navigation wireframes.

## Discussion
David shared the finalized warehouse codes: DEN (Denver), RNO (Reno), and the three
existing ones — SEA (Seattle, HQ), PDX (Portland), and BOI (Boise). Elena had been
mocking up placeholder names, so she'll swap in the real codes across all wireframes.

Elena presented the updated navigation: a warehouse switcher pinned to the top nav,
with a "compare all warehouses" view that David specifically requested after seeing
the single-warehouse-only mockups last month. Sarah liked the direction but asked
Elena to make the compare view the default landing page instead of a secondary tab,
since that's the view David's operations team will use most.

David also mentioned that Reno's warehouse manager, a new hire named Priya Nair,
should be looped into future syncs since she'll be the primary user of the multi-warehouse
view once it ships.

## Action Items
- Elena: Update wireframes with real warehouse codes (DEN, RNO, SEA, PDX, BOI).
- Elena: Promote the compare-all-warehouses view to the default landing page.
- Sarah: Add Priya Nair to the Project Lighthouse stakeholder distribution list.

2026-05-25, same time.
